chapter: Chapter 3: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

questions.question: Explain the conditions for a pair of linear equations to have a unique solution.
questions.answer: A pair of linear equations has a unique solution when their graphs intersect at exactly one point. Algebraically, this happens when a₁/a₂ ≠ b₁/b₂.

questions.question: Explain the conditions under which a pair of linear equations has no solution.
questions.answer: A pair of linear equations has no solution when their graphs are parallel. Algebraically, this happens when a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ ≠ c₁/c₂.

questions.question: Explain the conditions under which a pair of linear equations has infinitely many solutions.
questions.answer: A pair of linear equations has infinitely many solutions when their graphs coincide completely. Algebraically, this happens when a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ = c₁/c₂.

questions.question: Explain the consistency of a pair of linear equations.
questions.answer: A pair of linear equations is consistent if it has at least one solution. It may have either a unique solution or infinitely many solutions.

questions.question: Explain the inconsistency of a pair of linear equations.
questions.answer: A pair of linear equations is inconsistent if it has no solution. Graphically, this is represented by parallel lines.

questions.question: Explain the elimination method of solving a pair of linear equations.
questions.answer: In the elimination method, one variable is eliminated by making the coefficients equal and then adding or subtracting the equations to solve for the remaining variable.

questions.question: Explain the substitution method of solving a pair of linear equations.
questions.answer: In the substitution method, one variable is expressed in terms of the other using one equation and substituted into the second equation to find the solution.

questions.question: Explain the cross-multiplication method of solving a pair of linear equations.
questions.answer: The cross-multiplication method uses determinants to directly find the values of x and y when equations are in standard form.

questions.question: Explain why graphical method gives only approximate solutions.
questions.answer: Graphical method gives approximate solutions because accuracy depends on the scale of the graph and precision of plotting.

questions.question: Explain why algebraic methods are preferred over graphical method.
questions.answer: Algebraic methods are preferred because they provide exact solutions and are not affected by drawing inaccuracies.

questions.question: Explain the role of slope in determining the nature of solutions.
questions.answer: Slope determines whether lines intersect, are parallel, or coincide, which decides the number of solutions.

questions.question: Explain why two equations are necessary to solve two unknowns.
questions.answer: One equation alone cannot uniquely determine the values of two variables; hence two equations are required.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by dependent and independent systems.
questions.answer: An independent system has a unique solution, while a dependent system has infinitely many solutions.

questions.question: Explain how linear equations are used in real-life situations.
questions.answer: Linear equations are used in problems related to cost, distance, speed, time, and resource allocation.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of simultaneous equations with examples.
questions.answer: Simultaneous equations are equations solved together to find common solutions, such as x + y = 5 and x − y = 1.

questions.question: Explain the graphical interpretation of consistency.
questions.answer: Graphically, consistent systems are represented by intersecting or coincident lines.

questions.question: Explain the graphical interpretation of inconsistency.
questions.answer: Graphically, inconsistent systems are represented by parallel lines.

questions.question: Explain why cross-multiplication method fails for parallel lines.
questions.answer: For parallel lines, the determinant becomes zero, making the method invalid.

questions.question: Explain why coincident lines represent dependent equations.
questions.answer: Because one equation is a multiple of the other, making them dependent.

questions.question: Explain how ratio conditions help classify solutions.
questions.answer: Ratio conditions help determine whether equations have unique, no, or infinitely many solutions.

questions.question: Explain the importance of graphical representation in understanding solutions.
questions.answer: Graphs help visualize the nature of solutions and relationships between equations.

questions.question: Explain why linear equations model uniform change.
questions.answer: Because the rate of change remains constant.

questions.question: Explain how determinant helps in solving equations.
questions.answer: Determinant helps check whether a unique solution exists and is used in cross-multiplication.

questions.question: Explain why graphical solutions may differ slightly from algebraic solutions.
questions.answer: Because graphical solutions depend on drawing accuracy and scale.

questions.question: Explain the educational importance of studying pair of linear equations.
questions.answer: They form the basis for solving real-life problems and higher algebra.
